title: EEGEyeNet Dataset

funding_references.funder_name: Schweizerischer Nationalfonds zur Förderung der Wissenschaftlichen Forschung

description: EEGEyeNet is a simultaneous electroencephalography and eye-tracking dataset designed for eye movement prediction research. This BIDS-standardized dataset contains raw EEG and eye-tracking data collected during a controlled experimental paradigm where subjects performed tasks while their neural activity and eye movements were simultaneously recorded. The data serves as a benchmark for developing and evaluating machine learning models that predict eye movements from neural activity.
methods_description: Data were collected simultaneously using electroencephalography (EEG) and eye-tracking equipment during controlled experimental sessions. EEG was recorded using a standard clinical setup with multiple channels sampled at high temporal resolution. Eye-tracking data captured gaze position and pupil information synchronized with EEG recordings. The study received ethical approval from the Institutional Review Board of Canton Zurich (BASEC-Nr. 2017-00226). This dataset represents a BIDS-standardized subset from the larger EEGEyeNet benchmark study, with raw data preprocessed and organized according to BIDS conventions.
